title: Tools, Resources, Prompts, and Server Instructions · Integration Workshop
summary: Design a minimal MCP surface for an internal knowledge and action service. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an MCP primitive and naming contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Expose actions as narrow tools with descriptive schemas and side-effect annotations.
Expose readable addressable context as resources or templates rather than action tools.
Expose reusable user-controlled prompt templates only when they improve discovery and composition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

stimulus.title: Primitive Choice failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Primitive Choice condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Primitive Choice applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose the smallest primitive that preserves consent, discoverability, caching, and semantics.
